spec: Holo Desk (the substrate-native desktop) works in a real browser — right-click 'New ▸ Folder', inline-rename, drag-arrange, NEST (drop onto a folder), marquee multi-select, save any holospace app as a desktop icon + pin it to the native menu bar (dock), and folders whose cover updates to match their contents; OPFS-persisted + OS-adaptive; the κ-pinned icon library is natively discoverable and every icon is a UOR object; the desktop layout itself re-derives to its content address (Law L5)
authority: ADR-0061 (Holo Desk) · ADR-0058 (Holo Files VFS) · ADR-0059 (Holo Dock) · ADR-0032 (Holo Icons) · ADR-0057 (Holo UI) · W3C OPFS / File System Access · Web Crypto · verify by driving the real desktop in Chromium

results.name: the desktop surface boots (HoloDesk + #holo-desk + engines ready)
the desktop is OS-adaptive (data-holo-platform set → native folder/selection feel)
right-click opens the native context menu with "New"
a new folder icon appears on the desktop
the new folder opens an inline-rename field (edit name like native)
the rename persists to the writable OPFS Home (Law: Home is read/WRITE)
the κ-pinned icon library is natively discoverable (HoloIcons.names)
every icon is a content-addressed UOR object (did:holo)
an applied icon is stored as part of the layout
the desktop layout is itself a self-verifying UOR object (re-derives to its κ, Law L5)
any holospace app saves as a desktop icon (a .holospace shortcut, kind=app)
a desktop app can be pinned to the native menu bar (dock)
icons NEST — dropping an item onto a folder moves it inside
a folder's cover updates to MATCH its contents (preview tiles)
marquee rubber-band selects multiple icons at once
captured a screenshot of the working desktop
the desktop runs with no fatal errors of its own
